mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real GDP and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a negative rank IC greater than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of co-occurring declines.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sharp drop in the industrial production index reflects capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term market repricing, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of late-cycle observations, or the regime-conditional alpha vanishes when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy increases the cost of capital and slows economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of high-rate periods, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is replaced with a lagged moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling nonfarm payrolls indicates deteriorating labor market quality and hidden slack, which suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a rank IC above -0.02 over any 3-year window, or 60-day forward returns during divergence periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline market returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn regime, housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals collapsing construction activity and related consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when tested outside the specified housing regime.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing output and stronger corporate earnings momentum, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during production expansions are not significantly higher than baseline.
